header_summary:
In recent discussions within the Bitcoin development community, there has been significant consideration toward enhancing security against quantum computing threats by potentially disabling Elliptic Curve (EC) opcodes/paths in new output types like P2TRv2. Proposed mechanisms include the Tripwire, suggested by Tadge Dryja, which uses a specific transaction type as a trigger, and the Miner Lockdown, discussed by Sjors Provoost, which allows a majority of the mining hashrate to initiate disabling, thereby ensuring a quicker response to threats. These methods are integrated directly into the consensus rules to ensure reliable enactment when needed and align with the decentralized ethos by allowing user participation in critical decisions, thus enhancing network security and promoting the adoption of Post-Quantum Cryptographic methods. [More details on this discussion](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/yHRpA2LEJ2AugT4W2KiWO3ggSims4GuHBkr6rWtE-e1uVC2wh3ZqD4bUDyxXq1iEuPrezhZZeqDoG7uBLNvjbW0sk_UTorI1Pkz6LcKhXRA=@wuille.net/T/#u#m748a7fa3b9c284cae66474304b70d79ba5cd0845).

The intersection of blockchain technology and artificial intelligence, particularly in using the Bitcoin blockchain for executing AI-driven contract protocols, is gaining traction. Highlighted by a recent paper, this area of study emphasizes the potential and current limitations of real-world cryptography like functional homomorphic encryption, essential for secure and efficient contract management. Despite the complexity, this field continues to inspire academic and practical development, though deeper involvement is constrained by other commitments. [Read the detailed paper](https://dl.acm.org/doi/pdf/10.1145/3372297.3417278).

The ASmap dashboard, a new tool from the Summer of Bitcoin project, provides an interactive platform for tracking Bitcoin-core/asmap-data history, revealing the evolution of address space allocations and operator coverage. Developed under the guidance of mentors fjahr and jurraca, this dashboard includes features like a diff explorer and a novel evaluation method for .dat files, enhancing analysis of the network's structure over time. The developers seek community feedback to refine this tool further, emphasizing its potential utility in network analysis. [Explore the ASmap dashboard](https://jorisstrakeljahn.github.io/asmap-dashboard/).

recent_posts.title: Giving teeth to expected EC disabling: P2XX(-T)(-ML)
recent_posts.summary:
- Discussions in the Bitcoin community focus on enhancing security against quantum threats.
- Two proposed mechanisms, Tripwire and Miner Lockdown, aim to disable vulnerable EC opcodes.
- These changes, embedded in consensus rules, ensure timely protection without additional forks.

recent_posts.title: Accountable Computing On-Chain Contracts for AI Agents Supervision
recent_posts.summary:
- The Bitcoin blockchain's use in AI contract protocols is gaining academic and practical interest.
- Cryptography, especially functional homomorphic encryption, is in early development stages needing refinement.
- Continued research is essential for advancing blockchain's ability to handle AI-driven contracts securely.

recent_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
recent_posts.summary:
- The exploration of transaction privacy in Bitcoin is a critical focus in cryptocurrency.
- An informative thread on DelvingBitcoin.org discusses advancements and challenges in this area.
- A call for community engagement emphasizes the need for diverse insights on privacy issues.

recent_posts.title: Roast our real-time Bitcoin explorer built for everyday Bitcoin users
recent_posts.summary:
- Blockchain targets a ten-minute average for creating new blocks, maintaining network consistency.
- User interfaces show a countdown timer to represent the blockchain's operational "heartbeat."- The design focuses on clear and honest user understanding of network timing performance.

recent_posts.title: Addressing the Diminishing Block Subsidy
recent_posts.summary:
- Modifying Bitcoin's subsidy structure is complex due to valuation difficulties.
- An approach suggests dynamically adjusting block size based on demand and fees.
- Implementation could require a soft fork or more complex changes like a hard fork.

recent_posts.title: ASmap dashboard: tracking the asmap-data history against the observed network
recent_posts.summary:
- The ASmap dashboard, part of Summer of Bitcoin, visualizes bitcoin data history changes.
- The Network tab uses a scoring system to assess .dat file effectiveness against real nodes.
- Developers seek feedback on the dashboard's metrics, data clarity, and potential improvements.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] P2P UTXO Set Sharing
active_posts.summary:
- The BIP draft introduces a new update for sharing UTXO sets in the Bitcoin network.
- New P2P messages and a service bit will facilitate decentralized data transfers.
- The proposal enhances node setup time and network resilience by reducing reliance on external UTXO sources.

active_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal modifies BIP360, mandating at least one merkle path in P2MR control blocks.
- Aimed to cease depth-zero script trees in P2MR, increasing security and privacy.
- Feedback is sought, especially from P2TRv2 adherents, to evaluate impact and acceptance.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal introduces a new Bitcoin rule targeting specific 64-byte transaction structures.
- It focuses on preventing SPV malleability by invalidating blocks with certain Merkle tree preimages.
- Implementation advice includes a C++ function and guidelines for miners on block validation.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin privacy enhances through sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations, not Layer 1.
- Cross-Input Signature Aggregation could lower CoinJoin costs but won't improve privacy.
- Strategic focus favors regulatory-friendly solutions over fundamental protocol changes.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal introduces a recoverable EC leaf in BIP-360 to reduce EC witness size.
- This optimized method involves deriving the public key from the signature to decrease witness size.
- Technical modifications include changing the validation logic and enhancing nonce derivation for security.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts.summary: - SegData proposes efficient data handling in Bitcoin blocks via a soft-fork.  - The system allows pruning and shifts storage costs to operators choosing to retain data.  - It is designed for off-chain data services but not suitable for script-evaluated data types.

today_in_history_posts.title: Generalized covenant to implement side chains embedded into the bitcoin block chain
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Generalized covenants allow creation of federated side chains on the Bitcoin blockchain.
- Owners can transfer encumbered coins with additional Transfer signatures.
- Exit and Transfer signatures from federation members enforce unique transaction rules.

today_in_history_posts.title: [PROPOSAL]: FAST - Forked Away Simultaneous Transactions
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A proposal allows simultaneous payments through one transaction channel via repurposed onion blobs.
- Transaction efficiency increases with potential for reduced fees and more HTLCs in flight.
- However, this can lead to higher transaction failure risks due to dependency on multiple branches.

today_in_history_posts.title: Reimagining Onion Messages as an Overlay Layer
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Reimagining onion messaging in the LN promises faster adoption and enhanced privacy.
- The overlay's incremental deployment improves UX by reducing latency in payments.
- It supports rapid feature development and maintains compatibility with existing systems.
